---
title: "Unique genes"
format: html
editor: visual
author: 
- Weronika Oprzędek
---

## Libraries

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(tibble)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggrepel)
  library(pheatmap)
  library(optparse)
  library(readr)
  library(purrr)
  library(writexl)
  library(readxl)
})
```

# 1. unique genes

```{r}
plot_genes <- function(core_metrics, gene_name, segment = "all", output_dir,
    z_cutoff_uni = 2.5, z_cutoff_multi = 2.0, dist_quantile = 0.9){

  # assign segment type from gene name
  core_metrics <- core_metrics %>%
    mutate(segment_type = substr(gene, 4, 4))

  # optional filtering by segment
  if (segment != "all") {
    core_metrics <- core_metrics %>%
      filter(segment_type == segment)
  }

  segment_label <- ifelse(segment == "all", "all segments", 
                          paste(segment, "segments"))

  # build analysis matrix (gene-level features only)
  core_matrix <- core_metrics %>%
    column_to_rownames("gene") %>%
    mutate(
      # impute missing values with median of column
      mean_dnds = ifelse(is.na(mean_dnds),
                         median(mean_dnds, na.rm = TRUE), mean_dnds),
      mean_distance = ifelse(is.na(mean_distance), 
                             median(mean_distance, na.rm = TRUE), mean_distance)
    ) %>%
    select(snv_rate, indel_rate, sv_rate, total_contribution_len,
           mean_dnds, mean_distance, var_allele_count,
           diversity, var_diversity)

  # remove features with no variance
  sd_vec <- apply(core_matrix, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  core_matrix <- core_matrix[, sd_vec > 0]

  # stop early if not enough data for multivariate analysis
  if (ncol(core_matrix) < 2 || nrow(core_matrix) < 3) {
    msg <- paste0(gene_name, " ", segment,
                  ": too few data for multivariate analysis. ")
    if (ncol(core_matrix) < 2) msg <- paste0(msg, "Number of metrics = ", ncol(core_matrix), ". ")
    if (nrow(core_matrix) < 3) msg <- paste0(msg, "Number of genes = ", nrow(core_matrix), ".")
    warning(msg)
    return(NULL)
  }

  # standardize features for PCA + distance analysis
  core_scaled <- scale(core_matrix)

  # 1. Univariate outliers
  z_scores <- as.data.frame(core_scaled)
  z_scores$gene <- rownames(core_scaled)

  outliers_univariate <- z_scores %>%
    pivot_longer(-gene, names_to = "metric", values_to = "z") %>%
    filter(abs(z) >= z_cutoff_uni) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(z)))

  # 2. PCA decomposition
  pca <- prcomp(core_scaled, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)

  pca_scores <- as.data.frame(pca$x)
  pca_scores$gene <- rownames(pca_scores)

  # 3. Distance-based outliers
  dist_mat <- dist(core_scaled)
  dist_mean <- rowMeans(as.matrix(dist_mat))
  dist_z <- scale(dist_mean)[,1]

  distance_outliers <- tibble(
    gene = names(dist_mean),
    mean_feature_distance = dist_mean,
    z = dist_z
  ) %>%
    filter(
      mean_feature_distance >= quantile(mean_feature_distance, dist_quantile) &
      z >= z_cutoff_multi
    )

  # 4. Univariate summary per gene
  uni_max <- z_scores %>%
    pivot_longer(-gene, names_to = "metric", values_to = "z") %>%
    group_by(gene) %>%
    summarise(max_abs_z = max(abs(z)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(is_univariate = max_abs_z >= z_cutoff_uni)

  n_genes <- length(dist_mean)
  use_quantile <- n_genes >= 10

  # distance threshold logic
  dist_thr <- if (use_quantile) {
    quantile(dist_mean, dist_quantile)
  } else {
    -Inf
  }

  dist_df <- tibble(
    gene = names(dist_mean),
    mean_feature_distance = dist_mean,
    z_distance = scale(dist_mean)[,1]
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      percentile = ecdf(mean_feature_distance)(mean_feature_distance),
      quantile_used = use_quantile,
      is_quantile_outlier = mean_feature_distance >= dist_thr,
      is_z_outlier = z_distance >= z_cutoff_multi,
      is_multivariate = is_z_outlier & is_quantile_outlier
    )

  # 5. Combine all outlier definitions
  all_genes <- tibble(gene = rownames(core_scaled)) %>%
    left_join(uni_max, by = "gene") %>%
    left_join(
      dist_df %>% select(gene, mean_feature_distance, z_distance, percentile, is_multivariate),
      by = "gene"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      outlier_type = case_when(
        is_univariate & is_multivariate ~ "Multi",
        is_univariate ~ "Uni",
        is_multivariate ~ "Multi",
        TRUE ~ "None"
      )
    )

  # prepare output tables directory
  out_tbl_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "tables")
  if (!dir.exists(out_tbl_dir)) dir.create(out_tbl_dir, recursive = TRUE)

  # format table for export
  gene_table <- all_genes %>%
    mutate(
      is_univariate = ifelse(is_univariate, "Yes", "No"),
      is_multivariate = ifelse(is_multivariate, "Yes", "No")
    ) %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      max_absolute_z_score = max_abs_z,
      mean_multivariate_distance = mean_feature_distance,
      distance_z_score = z_distance,
      distance_percentile = percentile,
      univariate_outlier = is_univariate,
      multivariate_outlier = is_multivariate
    )

  # summary statistics
  summary_tbl <- gene_table %>%
    summarise(
      total_genes = n(),
      total_univariate_outliers = sum(univariate_outlier == "Yes"),
      total_multivariate_outliers = sum(multivariate_outlier == "Yes"),
      total_any_outliers = sum(outlier_type != "None")
    )

  print(summary_tbl)

  # export results to Excel
  sheets <- list(
    genes = gene_table,
    summary = summary_tbl
  )

  write_xlsx(
    sheets,
    file.path(out_tbl_dir,
              paste0(gene_name, "_", segment, "_outliers_tables.xlsx"))
  )

  # genes flagged as interesting
  interesting_genes <- all_genes %>%
    filter(outlier_type != "None") %>%
    select(gene)

  # 6. Output directories
  fig_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "graphs", "genes")
  if (!dir.exists(fig_dir)) dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE)

  # 7. Plot labels
  metric_labels <- c(
    snv_rate = "SNV rate",
    indel_rate = "INDEL rate",
    sv_rate = "SV rate",
    total_contribution_len = "Variability",
    mean_dnds = "Mean dN-dS",
    mean_distance = "Mean distance",
    var_allele_count = "Allele var",
    diversity = "Haplo div",
    var_diversity = "Haplo div region"
  )

  # 8. Heatmap

  # symmetric color scaling around zero
  max_abs <- max(abs(core_scaled), na.rm = TRUE)
  breaks <- seq(-max_abs, max_abs, length.out = 101)

  n_genes <- nrow(core_scaled)

  # adjust heatmap cell size based on number of genes
  cell_h <- ifelse(
    n_genes <= 30, 12,
    ifelse(n_genes <= 60, 9.5, 7)
  )

  colnames(core_scaled) <- metric_labels[colnames(core_scaled)]

  # annotation for heatmap rows
  row_annotation <- data.frame(
    Outlier = factor(
      all_genes$outlier_type[match(rownames(core_scaled), all_genes$gene)],
      levels = c("None", "Uni", "Multi")
    )
  )
  rownames(row_annotation) <- rownames(core_scaled)

  ann_colors <- list(
    Outlier = c(
      None = "#F0F0F0",
      Uni = "#E69F00",
      Multi = "#009E73"
    )
  )

  # heatmap output
  pheatmap(
    core_scaled,
    clustering_method = "ward.D2",
    labels_row = rownames(core_scaled),
    annotation_row = row_annotation,
    annotation_colors = ann_colors,
    angle_col = 45,
    fontsize_col = 8,
    fontsize_row = 8,
    cellheight = cell_h,
    color = colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "white", "#d7191c"))(100),
    breaks = breaks,
    main = paste(gene_name, segment, "gene variation landscape"),
    filename = file.path(fig_dir, paste0(gene_name, "_", segment, "_heatmap.png")),
    width = 6
  )

  # 9. PCA plot
  pca_var <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)
  pc1_var <- round(pca_var[1] * 100, 1)
  pc2_var <- round(pca_var[2] * 100, 1)

  p_pca <- ggplot(pca_scores, aes(PC1, PC2)) +
    geom_point(color = "grey70", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
    geom_point(data = pca_scores %>% filter(gene %in% interesting_genes$gene),
               color = "#d7191c", size = 2) +
    geom_text_repel(data = pca_scores %>% filter(gene %in% interesting_genes$gene),
                    aes(label = gene), size = 2,
                    box.padding = 0.3, point.padding = 0.25,
                    segment.color = "grey60") +
    theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
    labs(
      title = paste("PCA of gene-level metrics:", gene_name),
      subtitle = paste("Segment:", segment,
                       "| Z-cutoff univariate:", z_cutoff_uni,
                       "| Z-cutoff multivariate:", z_cutoff_multi,
                       "| Dist-quantile:", dist_quantile),
      x = paste0("PC1 (Explained variance: ", pc1_var, "%)"),
      y = paste0("PC2 (Explained variance: ", pc2_var, "%)")
    )

  ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, paste0(gene_name, "_", segment, "_PCA.png")),
         p_pca, width = 6, height = 5)

  # 10. Univariate outlier plot
  if (nrow(outliers_univariate) > 0) {

    outliers_univariate <- outliers_univariate %>%
      mutate(metric_label = metric_labels[metric]) %>%
      group_by(metric_label) %>%
      mutate(x_jitter = runif(n(), -0.2, 0.2)) %>%
      ungroup()

    p_uni <- ggplot(outliers_univariate, aes(x = x_jitter, y = z)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "#d7191c", size = 2) +
      geom_text_repel(aes(label = gene),
                      size = 2, max.overlaps = 50,
                      box.padding = 0.3, point.padding = 0.2) +
      facet_wrap(~ metric_label, scales = "free_x", ncol = 3) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = c(-z_cutoff_uni, z_cutoff_uni),
                 linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
      labs(
        title = paste("Univariate outliers:", gene_name),
        subtitle = paste("Segment:", segment, "| Z-cutoff:", z_cutoff_uni),
        y = "Z-score",
        x = NULL
      ) +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
      )

    ggsave(
      file.path(fig_dir, paste0(gene_name, "_", segment, "_univariate.png")),
      p_uni,
      width = 7,
      height = 5
    )

  } else {
    message(paste(
      gene_name, segment,
      ": no univariate outliers detected (z >", z_cutoff_uni, ")"
    ))
  }

  # return results for downstream use
  return(list(
    interesting_genes = interesting_genes,
    univariate_outliers = outliers_univariate,
    pca = pca,
    distance_outliers = distance_outliers,
    core_matrix = core_matrix
  ))
}
```

# 2. comparison of metrics

```{r}
# Prepare data for conservation analysis
prepare_conservation_data <- function(complete_data) {
  complete_data <- complete_data %>%
    mutate(
      z_score = scale(total_contribution_len),
      z_dnds = scale(mean_dnds),
      z_dist = scale(mean_distance)
    )
  
  complete_data <- complete_data %>%
    mutate(
      # Categorize genes based on quantiles
      human_var_level = case_when(
        total_contribution_len >= quantile(total_contribution_len, 0.66, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "var",
        total_contribution_len <= quantile(total_contribution_len, 0.33, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "cons",
        TRUE ~ "mid"
      ),
      # Squeezed z-scores for plotting
      z_score_squeezed = atan(z_score) / (pi / 2)
    )
  
  complete_data <- complete_data %>%
    filter(!(is.na(z_dnds) & is.na(z_dist))) %>%
    mutate(
      z_evo_raw = rowMeans(cbind(z_dnds, z_dist), na.rm = TRUE),
      z_evo_composite = atan(z_evo_raw) / (pi / 2),
      evo_composite_level = case_when(
        z_evo_composite >= quantile(z_evo_composite, 0.66, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "var",
        z_evo_composite <= quantile(z_evo_composite, 0.33, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "cons",
        TRUE ~ "mid"
      ),
      joint_class = case_when(
        human_var_level == "var"  & evo_composite_level == "var"  ~ "Human↑ Evo↑",
        human_var_level == "cons" & evo_composite_level == "cons" ~ "Human↓ Evo↓",
        human_var_level == "var"  & evo_composite_level == "cons" ~ "Human↑ Evo↓",
        human_var_level == "cons" & evo_composite_level == "var"  ~ "Human↓ Evo↑",
        TRUE ~ "Intermediate"
      )
    )
  return(complete_data)
}
```

### plot

```{r}
plot_composite_conservation_all_loci <- function(
  complete_data,
  output_dir = "part2/graphs/compare/"
) {

  if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  
  locus_colors_vis <- c(
    TRA = "#C00000",  # dark red
    TRB = "#8B6508",  # dark gold/brown
    IGH = "#006030",  # dark green
    IGK = "#5F3FBF",  # dark purple
    IGL = "#2F70B3"   # dark blue
  )

  p <- ggplot(
    complete_data,
    aes(
      x = z_evo_composite,
      y = z_score_squeezed,
      color = locus,
      shape = joint_class
    )
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.75, size = 2) +
    geom_text_repel(
      data = complete_data,
      aes(label = gene),
      size = 2.2,
      max.overlaps = Inf
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    labs(
      x = "Evolutionary variability (squeezed composite z-score)",
      y = "Human population variability (squeezed z-score)",
      color = "Locus",
      title = "Composite evolutionary vs human population variability across antigen receptor loci",
      shape = "Human vs evolutionary\nvariability level"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = locus_colors_vis) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(
      "Human↑ Evo↑"  = 16,  # filled circle
      "Human↓ Evo↓"  = 17,  # triangle
      "Human↑ Evo↓"  = 15,  # square
      "Human↓ Evo↑"  = 18,  # diamond
      "Intermediate" = 1    # open circle
    )) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.direction = "horizontal",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
    )

  ggsave(
    file.path(output_dir, "all_loci_composite_evolutionary_variability.png"),
    p,
    width = 14,
    height = 11,
    dpi = 300,
    limitsize = FALSE
  )

  print(p)
}
```

# 0. Set up

```{r}
#Argument parser with help
option_list <- list(
  make_option(c("-g","--gene"), type="character", default="IGL",
              help="Gene name, e.g. TRB"),
  
  make_option(c("-i","--input_dir"), type="character", default="part2",
              help=paste(
                "Input folder path. Expected structure:",
                "\n  tables/<GENE>.xlsx       -- gene level metrics",
                sep="\n")),
  
  make_option(c("-o","--output_dir"), type="character", default="part2",
              help="Output folder path")
)

opt_parser <- OptionParser(option_list = option_list)

#Parse arguments
args <- if (interactive()) character(0) else commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)
opt <- parse_args(opt_parser, args = args)

#Extract variables
gene_name <- opt$gene
input_dir <- opt$input_dir
output_dir <- opt$output_dir


#Print summary
cat("Gene:", gene_name, "\n")
cat("Input dir:", input_dir, "\n")
cat("Output dir:", output_dir, "\n")
```

# 1. unique genes

## Data processing

```{r}
in_tbl_dir <- file.path(input_dir, "tables")
all_gene_metrics <- read_csv(
  file.path(in_tbl_dir, paste0(gene_name, "_all_gene_metrics.csv"))
)

if (!is.null(all_gene_metrics) && nrow(all_gene_metrics) > 0){
  # Replace missing haplotype-related values with zeros
  all_gene_metrics <- all_gene_metrics %>%
    mutate(
      n_haplotypes        = replace_na(n_haplotypes, 0),
      diversity           = replace_na(diversity, 0)
    )
  
  all_gene_metrics_clean <- all_gene_metrics %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      gene_id = gene,
      
      gene_start_bp = Start,
      gene_end_bp = End,
      
      variant_impact_score = total_contribution,
      variant_impact_score_per_bp = total_contribution_len,
      
      n_unique_variants = n_variants,
      n_haplotype_instances = n_samples,
      
      n_snv_variants = SNV,
      n_indel_variants = INDEL,
      n_sv_variants = SV,
      
      snv_rate_per_bp = snv_rate,
      indel_rate_per_bp = indel_rate,
      sv_rate_per_bp = sv_rate,
      
      mean_pairwise_distance_to_homologs = mean_distance,
      median_pairwise_distance_to_homologs = median_distance,
      
      mean_dN_dS_to_homologs = mean_dnds,
      median_dN_dS_to_homologs = median_dnds,
      
      n_reference_genes_matched = n_compared,
      matched_reference_genes = matched_genes,
      
      total_unique_haplotypes_across_regions = n_haplotypes,
      
      regional_haplotype_shannon_diversity = diversity,
      
      sd_allele_count = sd_allele_count,
      var_allele_count = var_allele_count,
      max_allele_count = max_allele_count,
      min_allele_count = min_allele_count,
      mean_allele_count = mean_allele_count,
      
      mean_shannon_diversity_across_regions = mean_diversity,
      sd_shannon_diversity_across_regions = sd_diversity,
      var_shannon_diversity_across_regions = var_diversity,
      
      fraction_regions_with_haplotypes = fraction_regions_with_variants
    )
  
  out_tbl_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "tables")
  if (!dir.exists(out_tbl_dir)) dir.create(out_tbl_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  
  write_xlsx(
    list(all_gene_metrics = all_gene_metrics_clean),
    file.path(out_tbl_dir, paste0(gene_name, "_metrics.xlsx"))
  )

  # Define final core metric set for downstream analyses
  core_metrics <- all_gene_metrics %>%
    select(
      gene,
      
      #mutational burden
      snv_rate,
      indel_rate,
      sv_rate,
      total_contribution_len,
      
      #evolutionary metrics
      mean_dnds,
      mean_distance, # <- correlated (0.66) with mean_dnds but different sets of NA
      
      #allelic structure
      var_allele_count,
      
      #haplotype structural complexity
      diversity,
      
      #regional distribution of variation
      var_diversity
    )
  
  # Compute pairwise correlations between all numeric gene-level metrics
  cor_long_unique <- cor(core_metrics %>% select(where(is.numeric)),
    use = "pairwise.complete.obs") %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("metric_1") %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -metric_1, names_to  = "metric_2", 
                 values_to = "correlation") %>%
    filter(metric_1 < metric_2)   #remove self-correlations and duplicates

  # Extract strongly correlated metric pairs
  cor_threshold <- 0.7

  cor_strong <- cor_long_unique %>%
    filter(abs(correlation) >= cor_threshold) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(correlation)))
}
```

## unique genes

```{r}
# all segments
#plot_genes(core_metrics, gene_name, "all", output_dir)

#V
plot_genes(core_metrics, gene_name, "V", output_dir)

#J
plot_genes(core_metrics, gene_name, "J", output_dir)
```

# Create xlsx tables

## outliers

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: true

files <- list.files("part2/tables/", pattern = "_outliers_tables.xlsx", full.names = TRUE)

gene_tables <- lapply(files, function(f){
  read_xlsx(f, sheet = "genes")
})

names(gene_tables) <- gsub("_outliers_tables.xlsx", "", basename(files))

summary_tables <- lapply(files, function(f){
  
  seg <- gsub("_outliers_tables.xlsx", "", basename(f))
  
  read_xlsx(f, sheet = "summary") %>%
    mutate(dataset = seg)
})

summary_all <- bind_rows(summary_tables)

summary_total <- summary_all %>%
  summarise(
    dataset = "ALL",
    total_genes = sum(total_genes),
    total_univariate_outliers = sum(total_univariate_outliers),
    total_multivariate_outliers = sum(total_multivariate_outliers),
    total_any_outliers = sum(total_any_outliers)
  )

summary_all <- bind_rows(summary_all, summary_total)

summary_J <- summary_all %>%
  filter(grepl("_J$", dataset)) %>%
  summarise(
    total_genes = sum(total_genes),
    total_univariate_outliers = sum(total_univariate_outliers),
    total_multivariate_outliers = sum(total_multivariate_outliers),
    total_any_outliers = sum(total_any_outliers)
  ) %>%
  mutate(dataset = "ALL_J")

summary_V <- summary_all %>%
  filter(grepl("_V$", dataset)) %>%
  summarise(
    total_genes = sum(total_genes),
    total_univariate_outliers = sum(total_univariate_outliers),
    total_multivariate_outliers = sum(total_multivariate_outliers),
    total_any_outliers = sum(total_any_outliers)
  ) %>%
  mutate(dataset = "ALL_V")

summary_all <- bind_rows(summary_all, summary_J, summary_V)

summary_all <- summary_all %>%
  mutate(
    percentage_univariate = round(100 * total_univariate_outliers / total_genes, 1),
    percentage_multivariate = round(100 * total_multivariate_outliers / total_genes, 1)
  )

sheets <- c(
  list(summary = summary_all),
  gene_tables
)

write_xlsx(
  sheets,
  "part2/tables/outliers.xlsx"
)

```

## all metrics

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| include: true

files <- list.files("part2/tables/", pattern = "_metrics.xlsx", full.names = TRUE)

metrics <- lapply(files, function(f){read_xlsx(f)})

names(metrics) <- gsub("_metrics.xlsx", "", basename(files))

write_xlsx(
  metrics,
  "part2/tables/metrics.xlsx"
)
```
